report_metadata.report_title: Iran-based Cyber Actors Enabling Ransomware Attacks on US Organizations

campaign_name: AA24-241A – Iran-based ransomware-enabling operations against US and foreign organizations

domains.evidence_snippet: Use NGROK (ngrok[.]io) deployment to create outbound connections to a random subdomain
Check your systems for outbound web requests to files.catbox[.]moe and ***.ngrok[.]io

urls.evidence_snippet: Microsoft Windows PowerShell ISE to run the command Invoke-WebRequest with a URI including files.catbox[.]moe
Use NGROK ngrok[.]io deployment to create outbound connections to a random subdomain

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.evidence_snippet: Install "AnyDesk" remote access program as a backup access method

tools.name: Meshcentral
tools.evidence_snippet: Deployment of Meshcentral to connect with compromised servers for remote access

tools.name: Ligolo
tools.evidence_snippet: Use the open source tunneling tool Ligolo (ligolo/ligolo-ng)

tools.name: NGROK
tools.evidence_snippet: Use NGROK (ngrok[.]io) deployment to create outbound connections to a random subdomain

tools.name: Pay2Key
tools.evidence_snippet: the FBI has historically observed this actor conduct hack-and-leak campaigns, such as the late 2020 campaign known as Pay2Key

cves.evidence_snippet: these actors have been observed scanning IP addresses hosting Check Point Security Gateways, probing for devices potentially vulnerable to CVE-2024-24919

these actors have conducted mass scanning of IP addresses hosting Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS and GlobalProtect VPN devices. The actors were likely conducting reconnaissance and probing for devices vulnerable to CVE-2024-3400
Historically, this group has exploited organizations by leveraging CVE-2019-19781 and CVE-2023-3519 related to Citrix Netscaler
CVE-2022-1388 related to BIG-IP F5 devices

ttps.technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
ttps.evidence_snippet: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools T1562.001

summary: Iran-based cyber actors (Pioneer Kitten / Fox Kitten / UNC757, assessed as Iranian state-sponsored) have conducted high-volume network intrusion operations against US organisations in the education, finance, healthcare, defence, and local government sectors since 2017, as recently as August 2024. Initial access is gained by exploiting public-facing devices via CVEs targeting Citrix Netscaler, F5 BIG-IP, Ivanti VPNs, Palo Alto PAN-OS, and Check Point gateways. Actors deploy webshells, create local accounts, and establish persistence via scheduled tasks and DLL side-loading. They collaborate directly with ransomware affiliates (NoEscape, Ransomhouse, ALPHV/BlackCat), providing network access in exchange for ransom proceeds. Separately, the group conducts GOI-aligned espionage targeting Israeli and Azerbaijani organisations. Key tools include AnyDesk, Meshcentral, Ligolo, and NGROK. Associated IOCs include seven recent IPs and domains (January–August 2024) and five historical IPs and domains (2023).

timeline.event: Start of observed high-volume intrusion attempts by the Iranian cyber group against U.S. organizations
timeline.evidence_snippet: This advisory outlines activity by a specific group of Iranian cyber actors that has conducted a high volume of computer network intrusion attempts against U.S. organizations since 2017 and as recently as August 2024.

timeline.event: Previous FBI and CISA advisory published: Iran-Based Threat Actor Exploits VPN Vulnerabilities
timeline.evidence_snippet: This CSA... highlights similar activity from a previous advisory Iran-Based Threat Actor Exploits VPN Vulnerabilities that the FBI and CISA published on Sept. 15, 2020.

timeline.event: Pay2Key hack-and-leak campaign conducted against Israel-based cyber infrastructure
timeline.evidence_snippet: The FBI has historically observed this actor conduct hack-and-leak campaigns, such as the late 2020 campaign known as Pay2Key... the FBI assesses Pay2Key was an information operation aimed at undermining the security of Israel-based cyber infrastructure.

timeline.event: First observed use of api.gupdate.net infrastructure by the actors
Earliest historical IP infrastructure associated with the actors appears in Table 11
Start of recent IP activity window provided in Table 10
Mass scanning of PAN-OS and GlobalProtect VPN devices for CVE-2024-3400 begins
timeline.evidence_snippet: As of April 2024, these actors have conducted mass scanning of IP addresses hosting Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS and GlobalProtect VPN devices. The actors were likely conducting reconnaissance and probing for devices vulnerable to CVE-2024-3400.

timeline.event: Scanning of Check Point Security Gateways for CVE-2024-24919 observed
timeline.evidence_snippet: As of July 2024, these actors have been observed scanning IP addresses hosting Check Point Security Gateways, probing for devices potentially vulnerable to CVE-2024-24919.

timeline.event: Most recent observed IP and domain activity window closes (Table 10 indicators observed through August 2024)
Initial version of advisory AA24-241A issued
